assertions.text: Imports and uses DataflowEngine from rete-engine with editor.use(engine)
data() method on AddNode reads inputs with double-bracket array access pattern (e.g. inputs.left?.[0]?.[0]) rather than single-bracket
assertions.evidence: AddNode.data() typed as `inputs: { a?: number[]; b?: number[] }` and reads `inputs.a?.[0] ?? 0` — single bracket only. DataflowEngine actually passes `number[][]` (array of arrays), so this will return an array instead of a scalar, causing the addition to produce NaN.

assertions.text: ReactPlugin is initialized with { createRoot } argument (required for React 19)
assertions.evidence: `const render = new ReactPlugin<Schemes, AreaExtra>();` — createRoot is not imported or passed. Will fail at runtime with React 19.

assertions.text: Plugin wiring follows correct order: editor.use(area), area.use(connection), area.use(render)
assertions.evidence: Order is correct: editor.use(area) → area.use(connection) → area.use(render) → editor.use(engine).

assertions.text: engine.reset() is called before engine.fetch() to clear cached results
assertions.evidence: `engine.reset(); const output = await engine.fetch(add.id);` inside the compute callback.
